title: Calculus & Functions in R
description: Numerical differentiation, elasticity, Cobb-Douglas, and growth rates

snippets.title: Numerical Derivative
snippets.notes: Central difference formula is more accurate than forward difference.

snippets.title: Partial Derivatives
snippets.notes: Hold other variables constant when taking partial derivative.

snippets.title: Gradient Vector
snippets.notes: Gradient is vector of all partial derivatives. Zero gradient → critical point.

snippets.title: Point Elasticity
snippets.notes: ε = (dQ/dP)·(P/Q). Negative for demand. |ε|>1 is elastic.

snippets.title: Constant Elasticity Demand
snippets.code:
# Q = a·P^b has constant elasticity = b
demand_ce <- function(P) 100 * P^(-0.8)

# Verify at multiple prices
for (P in c(5, 10, 20)) {
  e <- elasticity(demand_ce, P)
  cat('P =', P, ': ε =', round(e, 3), '\n')
}
# All give -0.8
snippets.notes: Log-log specification: ln Q = ln a + b·ln P → elasticity = b (constant).

snippets.title: Cost Functions
snippets.notes: MC = dTC/dQ. AC minimized where MC = AC.

snippets.title: Revenue and Marginal Revenue
snippets.code:
# P = 100 - 2Q (inverse demand)
total_revenue <- function(Q) Q * (100 - 2*Q)
marginal_revenue <- function(Q) 100 - 4*Q

# Revenue max where MR = 0
Q_max_rev <- 100 / 4  # 25

plot(0:50, total_revenue(0:50), type = 'l')
abline(v = Q_max_rev, lty = 2)
snippets.notes: MR = dTR/dQ. For linear demand P = a - bQ, MR = a - 2bQ.

snippets.title: Cobb-Douglas Production
snippets.notes: MP_L = α·Q/L (constant share of APL). Output elasticity = α.

snippets.title: MRTS
snippets.code:
# MRTS = -MP_L/MP_K = -(α/β)·(K/L)
MRTS <- function(L, K, alpha = 0.3, beta = 0.7) {
  -(alpha/beta) * (K/L)
}

# At L=100, K=50
MRTS(100, 50)  # -(0.3/0.7)·(50/100) = -0.214

# Interpretation: Can give up 0.214 K per extra L
snippets.notes: MRTS is slope of isoquant. Diminishes as L/K rises (convex isoquants).

snippets.title: Returns to Scale
snippets.code:
# Scale all inputs by t, check output scaling
test_RTS <- function(t, L = 100, K = 50, alpha = 0.3, beta = 0.7) {
  Q1 <- cobb_douglas(L, K, alpha = alpha, beta = beta)
  Q2 <- cobb_douglas(t*L, t*K, alpha = alpha, beta = beta)
  return(Q2 / Q1)
}

t <- 2
ratio <- test_RTS(t)
cat('If t =', t, ', output scales by', ratio, '\n')
cat('RTS:', ifelse(ratio == t, 'CRS', 
                   ifelse(ratio > t, 'IRS', 'DRS')))

snippets.title: Isoquant Plotting
snippets.code:
# For Q = A·L^α·K^β, K = (Q/(A·L^α))^(1/β)
isoquant <- function(L, Q_target, A = 10, alpha = 0.3, beta = 0.7) {
  (Q_target / (A * L^alpha))^(1/beta)
}

L_range <- 10:200
plot(L_range, isoquant(L_range, 100), type = 'l',
     xlab = 'L', ylab = 'K', main = 'Isoquant Q = 100')
snippets.notes: Isoquant shows all (L,K) combinations producing same Q.

snippets.title: Continuous Growth
snippets.code:
# Y(t) = Y₀·e^(gt)
continuous_growth <- function(Y0, g, t) Y0 * exp(g * t)

# 3% growth for 20 years
Y <- continuous_growth(100, 0.03, 0:20)
plot(0:20, Y, type = 'l', main = 'Growth at 3%')

# Doubling time
T_double <- log(2) / 0.03  # 23.1 years
snippets.notes: Growth rate g = d(ln Y)/dt. Rule of 70: doubling time ≈ 70/g%.

snippets.title: Log Difference as Growth Rate
snippets.code:
# For small changes: % change ≈ Δ ln(Y)
Y <- c(100, 103, 106.09)

# Standard growth rate
growth_std <- diff(Y) / head(Y, -1)

# Log difference
growth_log <- diff(log(Y))

cbind(Standard = growth_std, Log = growth_log)
# Nearly identical for small changes
snippets.notes: Log differences additive: ln(Y2/Y1) = ln Y2 - ln Y1.

snippets.title: Symbolic Differentiation
snippets.code:
# R's built-in D() for symbolic derivatives
expr <- expression(3*x^2 + 2*x + 5)
D(expr, 'x')  # Returns: 3 * (2 * x) + 2

# Product rule automatically applied
expr2 <- expression(x^2 * exp(x))
D(expr2, 'x')
snippets.notes: D() returns expression. For functions, use Deriv package.

common_patterns.template:
profit <- function(Q) TR(Q) - TC(Q)
result <- optimize(profit, c(0, 100), maximum = TRUE)
Q_star <- result$maximum

debugging_tips.fix: Check domain; use pmax to ensure positive values

debugging_tips.cause: Forgot that demand elasticity is negative
debugging_tips.fix: Price elasticity of demand should be ≤ 0
